subject: Catch up: SSE Streaming for Real-Time AI Agents!
body:
Hello First Name,

This week's 🦄 ai that works session? We dove deep into "SSE Streaming: Building Real-Time AI Agents with Server-Sent Events"!

You can grab the full recording, code, and diagrams from the session right here on GitHub:
https://github.com/hellovai/ai-that-works

We covered a ton about building real-time site summarizers using Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming. Want a quick rundown?

First off, **why bother with streaming?** Well, it's a game-changer for user experience. Think about it: real-time progress updates, knowing exactly what your AI agent is up to, and even letting users jump in and tweak long-running tasks. Super powerful stuff!

Then we talked about **Server-Sent Events (SSE)**. This neat, one-way protocol is perfect for when your server needs to send a steady stream of updates to the client. For a lot of common real-time UI needs, it can actually be way simpler than setting up WebSockets.

And how do you **architect for streaming**? We dug into using `asyncio` for super efficient batched, parallel processing. Plus, we showed off how tools like BAML's `@stream.not_null` give you fine-grained control over exactly which data fields get streamed. For interactive stuff like canceling tasks, a database-backed approach is just way more solid for managing state than trying to rely on fleeting WebSocket connections.

So, if there's *one* big takeaway from the session, it's this: Streaming isn't just about getting LLM tokens out faster. It's a core architectural choice that opens up truly responsive UIs, gives you way better insight into what your agents are doing, and lets users actually interact with them. Basically, it makes your AI apps feel alive and much more intuitive.

Next week, we're diving into "Building a Software Factory using Eval-Driven Development" – seriously, it's so epic we couldn't fit it all into one session!
You can sign up here: [Sign up link here]

Got questions? Just hit reply on this email or hop over to our Discord: https://www.boundaryml.com/discord. We read every message! Happy coding 🧑‍💻

Vaibhav & Dex
call_to_action: Sign up for next week's session on Building a Software Factory using Eval-Driven Development
